answer:
**The storm-at-sea scene occurs during St. Andrew's voyage to Mermedonia aboard a ship crewed by Christ (disguised as the "Helmsman" or "Steersman" at the helm) and two angels posing as sailors.**[^1]

After boarding, the travelers are miraculously fed by one of the angels. Then a violent tempest arises: "the deep was disturbed, the whale-mere was stirred. The horn-fish played, gliding through the spear-sea, and the grey gull, slaughter-greedy, circled. The sky-candle darkened, **winds waxed wild, waves ground together**, streams were stirred up, the stays sang out, the sails were soaked. The sea-dread stood in threatening throngs. The thanes became fearful in spirit."[^2] Andrew's companions despair, believing they will not survive, unaware that Christ guides the vessel.[^3]

Andrew praises the helmsman (Christ) for his skill amid the chaos: "Never have I met a better seaman, more skilled in his craft... steering at the stern. The stream’s-welling stills, the surf beats the shore. This boat is so swift, it fares, foam-necked, most like a fowl, gliding on the ocean... It is most like as if it stood still on the shore of the land, where no storm can stir it, no wind can awaken it..."[^4] Christ responds humbly, attributing control of the storm-tossed sea to God's power over the waves, and Andrew recounts Christ's miracle of calming the Sea of Galilee as encouragement.[^5]

The storm abates as the mariners sleep (miraculously transported by angels), and they arrive safely.[^6]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, ch. 1, [¶24-32](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-24dbdc) (boarding; Christ as Helmsman: "sat himself near the Helmsman"; storm prelude). Full chapter: [ch. 1](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/).
[^2]: *Id.*, [¶46](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-8fd929).
[^3]: *Id.*, [¶46-47](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-8fd929).
[^4]: *Id.*, [¶52](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-ca9772).
[^5]: *Id.*, [¶52-54](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-ca9772) (Andrew's speech); [¶53](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-f3580c) (Christ's reply); [¶50](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-f66b6a) (Galilee miracle).
[^6]: *Id.*, [¶52](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-ca9772). Christ's identity as "steadfast Steersman" is later explicit: [¶82](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-02e4ce).
